docs.title: Home
docs.text:
Strands ADB

Give your agent a phone.

@tool decorated Android control for Strands Agents & DevDuck — drive any adb-connected Android device (phone, tablet, emulator) from an LLM.

docs.title: The Pitch
docs.text:
Your agent can already read files, call APIs, and run shell commands. Now it can also:

  • See the screen (screenshots come back as Converse API image blocks, not paths)
  • Tap & swipe — real UI automation
  • Read notifications, battery, sensors, thermals
  • Launch apps, open URLs, send SMS drafts, make calls
  • Take physical photos (yes, through the actual camera)
  • Stream logcat into your agent's event bus
  • Mutate settings — brightness, ringer, airplane mode, bluetooth

One tool. One verb: action=.... Works over USB or wireless adb.

docs.title: 2-Minute Quickstart
docs.text:
pip install strands-adb
brew install android-platform-tools   # adb on PATH
adb devices                           # plug in phone, accept USB debugging

from strands import Agent
from strands_adb import adb

agent = Agent(tools=[adb])
agent("take a screenshot of my phone and describe what's on screen")

→ Full Quickstart | Installation | Connect a Device

docs.title: 👁️ Agent Can SEE the Screen
docs.text:
screenshot returns a proper Converse API image block — the same format as strands_tools.image_reader. The agent doesn't just get a file path, it actually receives the pixels and reasons over them.

agent("take a screenshot and tell me what app is open")
# → adb(action="screenshot") returns PNG bytes in Converse image block
# → vision model reads it → "You're on the WhatsApp chat with Mom..."

graph LR
    A["🗣️ Agent"] -->|action=screenshot| B["📱 adb exec-out screencap"]
    B -->|PNG bytes| C["🖼️ Converse image block"]
    C -->|in context| D["👁️ Vision model"]
    D -->|"WhatsApp, chat w/ Mom"| A

graph TD

    ROUTER -->|screenshot| SCREEN["📸 screencap → PNG bytes"]

    DS --> PHONE

docs.title: Use Cases
docs.text:
• Personal phone assistant — "text mom back, she asked about dinner"
  • Accessibility agent — read notifications aloud, navigate apps via voice
  • Device monitoring — watch sensor/thermal streams, alert on anomalies
  • Automated testing — semantic UI automation for QA
  • Remote operation — control your phone from anywhere via SSH + wireless adb
  • Fleet management — one agent, many devices (multi-serial targeting)

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text:
The full adb tool signature. One tool, 90+ actions, dispatched via action="...".

from strands_adb import adb

result = adb(action="screenshot")

All actions return:

{
    "status": "success" | "error",
    "content": [{"text": "..."}, {"image": {...}}?],
    # action-specific extras
}

docs.title: screen_record
docs.text:
Blocking fixed-duration recording (≤180s Android hard limit).

adb(action="screen_record", duration_sec=30, output_path=None, serial=None)

docs.title: screen_record_start
docs.text:
Start a non-blocking background recording. Returns immediately so your agent can keep acting. Auto-chains segments past Android's 180s cap.

adb(
    action="screen_record_start",
    output_path="/tmp/run.mp4",
    screenrec_bit_rate_mbps=4,
    screenrec_size="720x1600",   # None = native resolution
    screenrec_segment_sec=180,
)

docs.title: screen_record_stop
docs.text:
Stop background recording. Pulls any remaining segment, optionally merges all segments via ffmpeg if present.

result = adb(action="screen_record_stop")
# {
#   "segments":   ["/tmp/run.mp4", "/tmp/run_seg002.mp4", ...],
#   "merged_path": "/tmp/run_merged.mp4",   # if ffmpeg available
#   "duration_sec": 412.3,
#   "total_bytes": 198_234_112,
# }

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text: How strands-adb is structured internally.

docs.title: Package Structure
docs.text:
strands_adb/
├── __init__.py                  # exports: adb
├── adb_tool.py                  # main @tool dispatch (90+ actions)
└── smart.py                     # smart_tap + UI helpers (XML parsing,
                                 # resource-id / content-desc matching)

One tool, one dispatch function, action-specific handlers.

docs.title: The Dispatch Pattern
docs.text:
graph TD
    AGENT["🗣️ Strands Agent"] -->|"@tool call: adb(action='screenshot', ...)"| TOOL["strands_adb.adb"]
    TOOL -->|match action| ROUTER{Router}

    ROUTER -->|list_devices| H1["_handle_list_devices"]
    ROUTER -->|screenshot| H2["_handle_screenshot"]
    ROUTER -->|smart_tap| H3["_handle_smart_tap"]
    ROUTER -->|camera_photo| H4["_handle_camera_photo"]
    ROUTER -->|...| HN["_handle_..."]

    H1 --> RUN["_run(args)"]
    H2 --> RUN
    H3 --> RUN
    H4 --> RUN
    HN --> RUN

    RUN -->|subprocess| ADB_BIN["adb binary"]
    ADB_BIN -->|USB/TCP| PHONE["📱 Android device"]
    PHONE -->|stdout| ADB_BIN
    ADB_BIN -->|result| RUN
    RUN -->|format| RESP["{status, content, ...}"]
    RESP --> AGENT

    style TOOL fill:#3DDC84,color:#000
    style PHONE fill:#3DDC84,color:#000

docs.title: Tool Surface
docs.text:
One @tool decorated function exposes all 90+ actions:

@tool
def adb(
    action: str,
    # Device targeting
    serial: Optional[str] = None,
    # Generic params
    x: int = None, y: int = None, text: str = None,
    package: str = None, url: str = None,
    # Screenshot / camera
    output_path: str = None, include_image: bool = True,
    # UI automation
    resource_id: str = None, content_desc: str = None,
    # Sensors / settings
    namespace: str = None, key: str = None, value: str = None,
    # ... (see api-reference.md for full list)
):
    """Android device control via adb."""
    ...

The LLM sees one tool with a clearly-documented action enum. Parameters are only required when their respective action needs them.

docs.title: The _run Core
docs.text:
Every action eventually calls:

def _run(args: List[str], serial=None, timeout=30):
    cmd = [_adb_bin()]
    if serial or _SELECTED_SERIAL:
        cmd += ["-s", serial or _SELECTED_SERIAL]
    cmd += args
    proc = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=timeout)
    return {"stdout": ..., "stderr": ..., "returncode": ...}
  • Serial selection priority: explicit arg → _SELECTED_SERIAL → $ADB_SERIAL → first available device
  • Timeout defaults to 30s (configurable)
  • Binary path via $ADB_BIN env

docs.title: The Converse API Bridge
docs.text:
Screenshot + camera actions return a content block compatible with AWS Converse API:

{
    "status": "success",
    "content": [
        {"text": "screenshot saved: /tmp/...png (284512 bytes)"},
        {"image": {"format": "png", "source": {"bytes": b"\x89PNG..."}}},
    ],
    "path": "/tmp/...png",
    "size_bytes": 284512,
}

Strands Agent passes this straight into the vision model's context. Same format as strands_tools.image_reader.

docs.title: Smart Tap Pipeline
docs.text:
smart.py handles semantic UI lookup:

sequenceDiagram
    participant T as adb tool
    participant P as Phone
    participant X as XML parser

    T->>P: uiautomator dump /sdcard/_ui.xml
    T->>P: cat /sdcard/_ui.xml
    P-->>T: <hierarchy>...</hierarchy>
    T->>P: rm /sdcard/_ui.xml
    T->>X: parse XML
    X->>X: find nodes matching criteria
    X->>X: priority: resource-id > text > content-desc > partial
    X-->>T: [{text, bounds, ...}]
    T->>T: compute center = ((x1+x2)/2, (y1+y2)/2)
    T->>P: input tap cx cy

docs.title: Camera Flow
docs.text:
sequenceDiagram
    participant A as Agent
    participant T as adb tool
    participant C as GoogleCamera
    participant D as /sdcard/DCIM

    A->>T: camera_photo, facing=front
    T->>D: ls -t (baseline)
    T->>C: am start STILL_IMAGE_CAMERA
    T->>C: wait 2s for viewfinder
    T->>C: smart_tap "Switch to front camera"
    T->>C: wait 2.5s
    loop retry 3x
        T->>C: tap shutter by resource-id
    end
    T->>D: poll every 500ms (max 15s)
    D-->>T: new IMG_*.jpg
    T->>T: adb pull → /tmp/
    T-->>A: image block (JPEG bytes)

docs.title: Logcat Streaming
docs.text:
graph LR
    subgraph "main thread"
        AGENT["🤖 Agent"]
    end

    subgraph "background thread"
        TAIL["adb logcat -v<br/>subprocess.Popen"]
        PARSE["parse line"]
        BUFFER["ring buffer"]
    end

    TAIL --> PARSE
    PARSE --> BUFFER
    PARSE -->|publish| BUS["DevDuck event_bus"]
    BUS -->|inject context| AGENT
  • Streaming is opt-in (log_stream_start)
  • Line-buffered subprocess
  • Parsed into structured events
  • Ring buffer (default N=500)
  • Each line → published to phone.logcat (or custom topic) on the event bus

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text: Variable Purpose Default ADB_BIN Path to adb binary adb (PATH) ADB_SERIAL Default device serial none (none others required)

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text:
Every handler returns:

# Success
{"status": "success", "content": [{"text": "..."}], ...}

# Error
{"status": "error", "content": [{"text": "descriptive error"}]}

Exceptions caught + converted. ADBError raised internally for clarity, then caught at tool boundary.

docs.text:
tests/
├── test_adb.py                 # action-level integration tests
├── test_smart.py               # smart_tap / UI matching
├── test_camera.py              # physical camera (requires device)
└── conftest.py                 # fixtures: mock + real device

11/11 tests passing on Pixel 10 Pro (Android 16).

python tests/test_adb.py

docs.title: Extending
docs.text:
Adding a new action:

  1. Add _handle_foo(...) in adb_tool.py
  2. Add elif action == "foo": branch in the dispatch
  3. Document parameters in the @tool docstring
  4. Add a test in tests/test_adb.py

That's it — no other wiring needed. The LLM will pick it up automatically once it's in the action list.

docs.title: Example: Autonomous Phone Agent
docs.text: A phone agent that runs 24/7 via DevDuck. Monitors notifications, responds to Telegram commands, optimizes device state, wakes you up.

docs.title: The Full Setup
docs.text:
# 1. Environment
export MODEL_PROVIDER=bedrock
export STRANDS_MODEL_ID="global.anthropic.claude-sonnet-4"
export AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK=xxx

export ADB_SERIAL="192.168.1.42:5555"           # wireless phone
export TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=xxx                   # for remote control
export DEVDUCK_AMBIENT_MODE=true
export DEVDUCK_AMBIENT_IDLE_SECONDS=60

export DEVDUCK_TOOLS="strands_adb:adb;strands_tools:shell,file_read,file_write,use_agent;devduck.tools:tasks,scheduler,ambient_mode,notify,telegram,system_prompt,identity"

# 2. Install as a service
devduck service install \
  --name phone-agent \
  --model "global.anthropic.claude-sonnet-4" \
  --startup-prompt "start logcat stream for notifications, then wait for telegram commands and ambient triage" \
  --env ADB_SERIAL=192.168.1.42:5555 \
  --env TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=$TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN \
  --env AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK=$AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK

Now phone-agent runs in the background, auto-restarts on failure, starts on boot.

docs.title: What It Does
docs.text:
graph TD
    START["🚀 Service boots"] --> LOG["📜 Start logcat stream"]
    LOG --> LISTEN["👂 Listen on Telegram"]
    LISTEN --> LOOP{Event loop}

    LOOP -->|ambient tick| AMBIENT["🌙 Ambient triage"]
    LOOP -->|telegram msg| TG["💬 Handle command"]
    LOOP -->|cron| CRON["⏰ Scheduled job"]

    AMBIENT --> TRIAGE["Classify notifs,<br/>surface urgent"]
    TG --> ACT["Execute request<br/>on phone"]
    CRON --> BRIEF["Morning brief"]

    TRIAGE --> NOTIFY["📲 Telegram alert"]
    ACT --> REPLY["💬 Telegram reply"]
    BRIEF --> NOTIFY

    style START fill:#3DDC84,color:#000

docs.title: Scheduled Jobs
docs.text:
Once the service is running, add recurring jobs:

scheduler(action="add",
  name="morning-brief",
  schedule="0 8 * * *",
  prompt="read notifications from last 12h, send telegram brief")

scheduler(action="add",
  name="thermal-check",
  schedule="*/5 * * * *",
  prompt="check thermals; if CPU > 80°C reduce brightness")

scheduler(action="add",
  name="battery-watch",
  schedule="*/15 * * * *",
  prompt="check battery; if <15% and not charging, telegram warn me")

docs.title: Telegram Commands
docs.text:
With devduck.tools:telegram in your DEVDUCK_TOOLS, the agent listens for messages. Send from your phone:

  • status → device info, battery, thermals, current app
  • screenshot → agent takes a screenshot + sends image back
  • open spotify → launches Spotify
  • read last mom msg → reads WhatsApp
  • airplane mode → enables it
  • where's the phone? → last known location / on-desk / in-pocket

The agent interprets freely because it has the full adb toolbelt.

docs.title: Identity for Multiple Phones
docs.text:
If you control multiple devices, create identities:

identity(action="create",
  name="pixel-main",
  system_prompt="You manage the main Pixel phone at 192.168.1.42",
  env_vars='{"ADB_SERIAL": "192.168.1.42:5555"}',
  tools_config="strands_adb:adb;strands_tools:shell",
  model_id="global.anthropic.claude-sonnet-4")

identity(action="create",
  name="tablet-couch",
  system_prompt="You manage the Samsung tablet in the living room",
  env_vars='{"ADB_SERIAL": "10.0.0.50:5555"}',
  tools_config="strands_adb:adb;strands_tools:shell",
  model_id="global.anthropic.claude-sonnet-4")

# Fan out
identity(action="fan_out", system_knowledge='''[
  {"identity": "pixel-main",  "task": "check notifications"},
  {"identity": "tablet-couch", "task": "keep brightness adaptive"}
]''')

Now one DevDuck instance orchestrates multiple phones.

docs.title: Autonomous Mode
docs.text:
For self-directed work — the agent plans and executes until a task is truly done:

devduck
🦆 auto
🦆 investigate: my phone drops wifi every hour around 9pm. diagnose.

Agent will:

  1. Read wifi_info now + in loop
  2. Check logcat for wifi errors
  3. Hypothesize (router? range? interference?)
  4. Toggle settings to test
  5. Report findings

→ DevDuck autonomous mode

docs.title: Monitoring the Agent
docs.text:
devduck service logs --name phone-agent --follow
devduck service status --name phone-agent

docs.title: Example: Notification Triage
docs.text: Stream notifications from the phone. Agent decides which are urgent, surfaces them.

docs.title: What It Does
docs.text:
1. Start a background logcat stream filtering for NotificationManagerService
  2. DevDuck's event bus receives every new notification
  3. Agent periodically (or ambient-mode) reviews buffered events
  4. For important ones → notify() to macOS / Telegram / speak
  5. Dismiss trivial ones

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
export DEVDUCK_TOOLS="strands_adb:adb;strands_tools:shell;devduck.tools:notify,telegram,ambient_mode,scheduler"
export DEVDUCK_AMBIENT_MODE=true
export TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=xxx

docs.title: Start the Stream
docs.text:
# On first DevDuck prompt:
🦆 start logcat stream for notifications, filter NotificationManagerService

This calls under the hood:

adb(action="log_stream_start",
    filter="NotificationManagerService",
    topic="phone.notifications")

docs.title: Ambient Triage
docs.text:
With DEVDUCK_AMBIENT_MODE=true, the agent wakes up every minute of idle:

# Prompt (set via system_prompt or initial query):
🦆 every time you wake, check the event bus for phone.notifications.
   for each notification:
     - if from Mom/Dad/Partner → notify me via Telegram + macOS
     - if from Slack or email → add to a daily digest
     - if spam (promo, system, ads) → ignore

graph LR
    LOG["📜 logcat"] -->|stream| ADB["strands_adb"]
    ADB -->|publish| BUS["🚌 event_bus<br/>phone.notifications"]
    BUS -->|inject context| AGENT["🤖 Ambient agent"]

    AGENT -->|urgent?| DECIDE{Classify}
    DECIDE -->|yes| NOTIFY["📱 notify()"]
    DECIDE -->|yes| TG["💬 telegram"]
    DECIDE -->|digest| DAILY["📝 append to digest"]
    DECIDE -->|spam| NOOP["🔇 ignore"]

    style AGENT fill:#3DDC84,color:#000

docs.title: Morning Brief via scheduler
docs.text:
scheduler(action="add",
  name="morning-brief",
  schedule="0 8 * * *",
  prompt="""
  read phone.notifications events from the last 12 hours.
  write a morning brief: who tried to reach me, what was urgent,
  what's my todo list based on these messages.
  notify me via Telegram.
  """,
  tools="strands_adb.adb,devduck.tools.telegram,devduck.tools.notify")

docs.title: One-Shot Variant (no streaming)
docs.text:
Don't want background streams? Just poll:

agent("""
dump current notifications via notifications_parsed.
surface anything from Mom/Dad/Partner that's unread.
""")

docs.title: Dismissing
docs.text:
adb(action="dismiss_notifications")    # dismiss all
adb(action="key", key="KEYCODE_ESCAPE") # dismiss the shade

docs.title: Full Example Script
docs.text:
# examples/notifications.py
from strands import Agent
from strands_adb import adb

agent = Agent(
    tools=[adb],
    system_prompt="""
You are a notification triage agent.
- Use notifications_parsed to see structured notifications.
- Classify: URGENT (family/partner), IMPORTANT (work), DIGEST, SPAM.
- For URGENT: return a clear summary.
- For SPAM: suggest dismiss.
""",
)

while True:
    result = agent("check current notifications, triage them")
    print(result)
    input("\nPress enter to check again...")

docs.title: Privacy
docs.text:
Logcat exposes notification content including message previews. Treat accordingly:

  • Filter aggressively (NotificationManagerService only)
  • Redact sensitive fields before logging
  • Don't push raw notification content to third-party APIs without thinking

→ See Safety guide.

docs.title: Example: Screen Reader
docs.text: Continuously describe what's on screen. Perfect for screen-free workflows, accessibility, or monitoring.

docs.title: What It Does
docs.text:
Every N seconds:

  1. screenshot the phone
  2. Vision model reads what's on screen
  3. Speak / notify / log the description
  4. Repeat

docs.title: Minimal Version
docs.text:
import time
from strands import Agent
from strands_adb import adb

agent = Agent(
    tools=[adb],
    system_prompt="You describe phone screens concisely in 1-2 sentences.",
)

while True:
    print(agent("take a screenshot and describe what's on screen"))
    time.sleep(10)

docs.title: DevDuck Ambient Version
docs.text:
export DEVDUCK_TOOLS="strands_adb:adb;strands_tools:shell;devduck.tools:ambient_mode,notify,speak"
export DEVDUCK_AMBIENT_MODE=true
export DEVDUCK_AMBIENT_IDLE_SECONDS=10

devduck "every 10s, screenshot my phone and describe what's on screen, speak it aloud via 'say'"

With DEVDUCK_AMBIENT_MODE, the loop runs when you're idle. No need to manage threads.

docs.title: Continuous + Change Detection
docs.text:
Only announce when the screen meaningfully changed:

from strands import Agent
from strands_adb import adb
import time

agent = Agent(
    tools=[adb],
    system_prompt="""
Compare the new screenshot to the previous description.
If substantially the same, respond exactly: NO_CHANGE
Otherwise describe what's new in 1 sentence.
""",
)

last = ""
while True:
    result = str(agent(f"""
    Previous: {last}
    New screenshot coming. Compare to previous and report.
    """))
    if "NO_CHANGE" not in result:
        print(result)
        last = result
    time.sleep(5)

docs.title: Accessibility Use Case
docs.text:
For vision-impaired users — pair with speak:

export DEVDUCK_TOOLS="strands_adb:adb;strands_tools:shell,speak"

devduck "every 5s, screenshot and describe any NEW content. speak it aloud."

docs.title: Performance
docs.text:
• Screenshot via exec-out screencap: ~200-300ms
  • Vision model inference: 1-3s (Bedrock Claude Sonnet 4) or 500ms (MLX Qwen3-VL on-device)
  • Total loop: ~3-4s per cycle → easy at 10s intervals

docs.title: Record-the-day mode
docs.text:
agent("""
start a screen frame capture at 1 fps for the next hour.
save to /tmp/my_day/.
""")
# → screen_frames action

Then process offline:

# Pipeline: frames → strands-cosmos for video understanding
# https://github.com/cagataycali/strands-cosmos

agent("""
enable live captions (accessibility), screenshot every 2s,
extract caption text, save to /tmp/meeting.txt.
""")

from strands import Agent
from strands_adb import adb

INTERVAL = int(sys.argv[1]) if len(sys.argv) > 1 else 10

docs.title: Example: WhatsApp Assistant
docs.text: Read the latest message from Mom. Draft a reply. Confirm before sending.

docs.title: What It Does
docs.text:
1. Launch WhatsApp
  2. Screenshot the chat list
  3. Find the chat from "Mom"
  4. Open that chat
  5. Screenshot, read the last message
  6. Draft a reply based on context
  7. Show draft to user (don't send yet)
  8. On confirmation, send

docs.title: The Agent
docs.text:
from strands import Agent
from strands_adb import adb

agent = Agent(
    tools=[adb],
    system_prompt="""
You are a WhatsApp assistant. When asked to reply, ALWAYS:
1. First read the message carefully (screenshot + vision)
2. Draft a reply
3. Show the draft and WAIT for explicit user confirmation
4. Only send after 'send it' / 'yes' / confirmation
Never send without confirmation.
""",
)

response = agent("""
open whatsapp, find the chat with Mom, read her last message,
and draft a reply. don't send yet — just show me the draft.
""")
print(response)

docs.title: Under the Hood
docs.text:
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as 🧑 User
    participant A as 🤖 Agent
    participant T as 🔧 adb
    participant W as 💬 WhatsApp

    U->>A: "open whatsapp, read mom's msg"
    A->>T: launch com.whatsapp
    T->>W: am start
    A->>T: screenshot
    T-->>A: image block (chat list)
    Note over A: vision: "mom chat is 3rd row"
    A->>T: smart_tap text="Mom"
    T->>W: uiautomator → tap
    A->>T: screenshot
    T-->>A: image block (chat)
    Note over A: vision: "mom wrote: 'dinner at 7?'"
    A-->>U: "Draft: 'works for me, see you then'<br/>Send? (y/n)"
    U->>A: "send it"
    A->>T: smart_tap text="Message"
    A->>T: type_text "works for me..."
    A->>T: smart_tap content_desc="Send"
    A-->>U: "✅ sent"

docs.title: Running
docs.text:
export DEVDUCK_TOOLS="strands_adb:adb;strands_tools:shell"

devduck "open whatsapp, read mom's latest, draft a reply, confirm before sending"

docs.title: Auto-reply to specific contacts only
docs.text:
ALLOWED = {"Mom", "Dad", "Partner"}

def safe_reply(agent, contact):
    if contact not in ALLOWED:
        return f"Refusing to auto-reply to {contact}"
    return agent(f"reply to {contact} with 'got it, ty'")

docs.title: Multi-platform (WhatsApp / Telegram / SMS)
docs.text:
agent("""
check all my messaging apps (WhatsApp, Telegram, SMS)
and summarize unread conversations by app.
""")

docs.title: Smart reply with context
docs.text:
agent("""
read the last 5 messages from mom, understand the topic,
and draft a contextual reply (not just 'ok').
don't send — show draft.
""")

docs.title: Safety Notes
docs.text:
• Never auto-send — always confirm
  • Treat every contact's text as untrusted input (prompt injection risk)
  • Consider allowlisting contact names
  • Log every draft to an audit file

→ See Safety guide for production hardening.

docs.title: Connect a Device
docs.text: Get your phone talking to adb. Three modes: USB, wireless, SSH-over-adb.

docs.title: Mode 1: USB (simplest)
docs.text:
1. Enable Developer Options: Settings → About phone → tap Build number 7 times
  2. Enable USB debugging: Settings → System → Developer options → USB debugging
  3. Plug in USB cable. Accept the trust dialog on the phone.
  4. Verify:
adb devices
# 59230DLCH0012Z  device

unauthorized?

If adb devices shows unauthorized, unlock the phone and accept the dialog. Still stuck? adb kill-server && adb start-server.

docs.title: Mode 2: Wireless adb (no cable after pairing)
docs.text: Android 11+ supports native wireless debugging.

docs.title: Pair once
docs.text:
1. Settings → Developer options → Wireless debugging → toggle on
  2. Tap Pair device with pairing code — note the IP, port, and 6-digit code.
  3. On your host:
adb pair 192.168.1.42:41337
# Enter pairing code: 123456
# Successfully paired

docs.title: Connect
docs.text:
adb connect 192.168.1.42:5555
adb devices
# 192.168.1.42:5555  device

docs.title: Make it stick
docs.text:
Add to ~/.zshrc / ~/.bashrc:

alias phone="adb connect 192.168.1.42:5555"

→ Details: SSH_ANDROID.md

docs.title: Mode 3: SSH over adb (remote agent → phone)
docs.text: You want your DevDuck running on a remote box (Jetson, VPS) to control a phone sitting on your desk. Two subsolutions:

docs.title: 3a. adb over SSH tunnel
docs.text:
On the host with the phone:

adb -a -P 5037 nodaemon server &   # listen on all interfaces

On the remote box:

ssh -L 5037:localhost:5037 user@host-with-phone
adb devices   # sees the remote phone

docs.title: 3b. adb running on the phone itself (Termux)
docs.text:
Install Termux on the phone, then:

pkg install android-tools openssh
sshd
adb tcpip 5555
adb connect localhost:5555

SSH into the phone from your agent's host, run commands there.

→ Full walkthrough: SSH_ANDROID.md

docs.title: Multi-Device Targeting
docs.text: Got more than one phone connected?

docs.title: Option A: Set a default globally
docs.text:
export ADB_SERIAL=59230DLCH0012Z

All adb(...) calls will target that device unless overridden.

docs.title: Option B: Per-call override
docs.text:
adb(action="screenshot", serial="59230DLCH0012Z")
adb(action="battery",    serial="emulator-5554")

docs.title: Option C: Let the agent choose
docs.text:
agent("list all connected devices, then take a screenshot of the Pixel")
# Agent calls list_devices → sees serials + models → picks the right one

docs.title: Custom adb Binary
docs.text:
If adb is not on PATH (or you want a specific version):

export ADB_BIN=/opt/android-sdk/platform-tools/adb

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: Symptom Fix adb: command not found Install android-platform-tools (see Installation) device unauthorized Accept the USB debug dialog on the phone no devices after reboot adb kill-server && adb start-server Wireless drops after suspend Reconnect with adb connect IP:5555 (script it) Screenshot is black Phone is locked — try adb(action="wake") first Permission denied on push Target a world-writable path like /sdcard/

    style V fill:#1e3a5f,stroke:#60a5fa,color:#fff
    style SM fill:#4a1d96,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#fff
    style DD fill:#92400e,stroke:#fbbf24,color:#fff

docs.title: Accessibility
docs.text: Android's Accessibility service is the most powerful — and most dangerous — API on the phone. strands-adb exposes a safe subset.

docs.title: List Services
docs.text:
adb(action="accessibility_list")
# → enabled services + all installable services

docs.title: Toggle a Service
docs.text:
adb(action="accessibility_toggle_service",
    service="com.google.android.accessibility.talkback/.TalkBackService",
    enabled=True)

Needs permission

Toggling services requires WRITE_SECURE_SETTINGS: 

adb shell pm grant <pkg> android.permission.WRITE_SECURE_SETTINGS

docs.title: System Actions
docs.text:
Trigger system-wide accessibility actions:

adb(action="accessibility_system_action", id="home")
adb(action="accessibility_system_action", id="back")
adb(action="accessibility_system_action", id="notifications")
adb(action="accessibility_system_action", id="quick_settings")

docs.title: Live Captions
docs.text: adb(action="accessibility_captions", enabled=True)

docs.title: Screen Magnification
docs.text: adb(action="accessibility_magnification", enabled=True, scale=2.0)

docs.title: Font Scale
docs.text:
adb(action="accessibility_font_scale", scale=1.3)   # 130% text size

Valid scales: 0.85, 1.0, 1.15, 1.3, 1.45, 1.6, 1.75, 2.0.

docs.title: Status
docs.text:
Overall accessibility state:

adb(action="accessibility_status")
# → {
#     "enabled_services": [...],
#     "touch_exploration": False,
#     "high_contrast": False,
#     "color_correction": "disabled",
#     "font_scale": 1.0,
#     "magnification_enabled": False,
#   }

docs.title: "Accessibility mode"
docs.text:
agent("""
enable TalkBack, live captions, and bump font to 1.3x.
My grandma is using the phone today.
""")

docs.title: "Back off the magnifier"
docs.text: agent("disable magnification and reset font to 1.0")

docs.title: Richer Screen Reading — Companion APK
docs.text: For true accessibility-tree reading (much richer than UIAutomator), you'd need a companion APK that registers as an AccessibilityService. This is tracked in FRONTIERS.md #2. ADB alone can toggle services but can't read their output without a helper app.

docs.title: What's Next
docs.text:
• Smart Tap — alternative to a11y for interaction
  • Settings — related a11y settings
  • Safety — why a11y is powerful and dangerous

docs.title: Actions Overview
docs.text:
strands-adb exposes one tool: adb. It dispatches on a single action="..." argument. There are 90+ actions grouped into domains.

from strands_adb import adb

adb(action="screenshot")
adb(action="smart_tap", text="Send")
adb(action="battery")

docs.title: Action Groups
docs.text:
graph TD
    ADB["🔧 adb(action=...)"] --> DEVICE["📱 Device"]
    ADB --> UI["🎯 UI"]
    ADB --> SCREEN["👁️ Screen"]
    ADB --> APPS["📦 Apps"]
    ADB --> FILES["📂 Files"]
    ADB --> INTENT["🔗 Intents"]
    ADB --> SENSOR["🌡️ Sensors"]
    ADB --> SETTINGS["⚙️ Settings"]
    ADB --> LOGS["📜 Logs"]
    ADB --> A11Y["♿ Accessibility"]
    ADB --> CAMERA["📸 Camera"]
    ADB --> COMMS["📞 Comms"]

    style ADB fill:#3DDC84,color:#000

docs.title: 📱 Device
docs.text: Action Purpose list_devices Enumerate connected devices select_device Set default device serial device_info Model, manufacturer, Android version battery dumpsys battery wake Wake screen unlock Wake + swipe + optional PIN

docs.title: 🎯 UI Input
docs.text: Action Purpose tap Tap at (x, y) swipe Swipe between two points over duration type_text Type text into focused field key Send keyevent (back, home, enter, volume_up, ...) back / home / recent Convenience keyevents gesture_long_press Long press at (x, y) gesture_path Multi-point gesture sequence gesture_pinch Pinch-to-zoom gesture_stream Stream of gestures

docs.title: 👁️ Screen & UI Inspection
docs.text:
Action Purpose screenshot Capture PNG + return as Converse image block screen_record Blocking fixed-duration recording screen_record_start / _stop / _status Background recording — record while agent acts screen_frames Extract N live screenshots at intervals video_frames Extract frames from an on-device video ui_dump Raw UIAutomator XML ui_find Find UI node by text / resource-id / content-desc ui_tap_by Tap a node found by ui_find criteria ui_wait_for Poll until a node appears smart_tap High-level: tap by semantic text

→ Vision guide · Smart tap guide · UI automation

docs.title: 📦 Apps
docs.text:
Action Purpose list_packages List installed apps launch am start a package kill Force-stop a package install adb install an APK uninstall Uninstall a package clear_data Clear app data current_app What's in the foreground

→ Apps & Intents guide

docs.title: 📂 Files
docs.text: Action Purpose push Host → device pull Device → host ls List remote directory

docs.title: 🔗 Intents
docs.text: Action Purpose open_url am start -a VIEW -d URL share_text Android share sheet start_activity Arbitrary am start with args

docs.title: 🌡️ Sensors & Thermals
docs.text:
Action Purpose sensors Accelerometer, gyro, light, etc. thermals CPU / skin / battery temps wifi_info SSID, BSSID, signal

→ Sensors guide

docs.title: ⚙️ Settings Mutation
docs.text:
Action Purpose setting_get / setting_put / setting_delete / setting_list Raw namespace-scoped settings set_ringer silent / vibrate / normal set_brightness 0–255 set_bluetooth enable/disable set_airplane_mode enable/disable setting_dump Dump all settings in a namespace

→ Settings guide

docs.title: 📜 Logs
docs.text:
Action Purpose logcat One-shot logcat -d log_stream_start / stop / status Background streaming → DevDuck event bus notifications / notifications_parsed Dump notifications (raw / structured)

→ Logcat streaming guide

docs.title: ♿ Accessibility
docs.text:
Action Purpose accessibility_list List available services accessibility_toggle_service Enable/disable a service accessibility_system_action Trigger a system action accessibility_captions Toggle live captions accessibility_magnification Enable screen magnification accessibility_font_scale Set font size multiplier accessibility_status Overall a11y state

→ Accessibility guide

docs.title: 📸 Camera
docs.text:
Action Purpose camera_photo Take a still photo (returns image block) camera_video Record a video

→ Camera guide

docs.title: 📞 Comms
docs.text: Action Purpose dial Open dialer with number (or auto-call) sms_compose Draft an SMS media_control play/pause/next/prev/stop volume Raise/lower/mute

docs.title: Response Shape
docs.text:
Every action returns:

{
    "status": "success" | "error",
    "content": [
        {"text": "human-readable summary"},
        # optionally, for screenshots / camera:
        {"image": {"format": "png", "source": {"bytes": b"..."}}},
    ],
    # action-specific extras:
    "path": "/tmp/foo.png",
    "devices": [...],
    "info": {...},
}

The content field follows the AWS Converse API content block format, which Strands Agent consumes directly.

docs.title: What's Next
docs.text:

  • Smart Tap — semantic UI automation
  • DevDuck — production agent runtime
  • API Reference — full action signatures

docs.title: Apps & Intents
docs.text: Launch apps, manage packages, and send Android intents.

docs.title: Launch an App
docs.text:
adb(action="launch", package="com.whatsapp")
adb(action="launch", package="com.spotify.music")
adb(action="launch", package="com.android.settings")

The tool calls am start -n <package>/<MAIN_ACTIVITY> under the hood.

docs.title: What's Currently Open
docs.text:
adb(action="current_app")
# → {"package": "com.whatsapp", "activity": "..."}

docs.title: List Installed Packages
docs.text:
adb(action="list_packages")                 # all packages
adb(action="list_packages", pattern="whats") # filter
adb(action="list_packages", third_party=True) # skip system apps

docs.title: Install / Uninstall
docs.text:
adb(action="install", apk_path="/path/to/app.apk")
adb(action="uninstall", package="com.foo.bar")

Destructive

uninstall is permanent. Use allowlists in production.

docs.title: Force-Stop / Clear Data
docs.text:
adb(action="kill", package="com.flaky.app")
adb(action="clear_data", package="com.broken.app")

docs.title: Open a URL
docs.text:
adb(action="open_url", url="https://news.ycombinator.com")

Equivalent to am start -a android.intent.action.VIEW -d <url>. The OS picks the right handler (browser, Spotify link, whatever).

docs.title: Share Text
docs.text:
Trigger the share sheet:

adb(action="share_text", text="Check this out: https://example.com")

docs.title: Arbitrary Activity
docs.text:
Full control:

adb(action="start_activity",
    action_name="android.intent.action.VIEW",
    data="content://contacts",
    package="com.google.android.contacts")

docs.title: "Pick up where I left off"
docs.text:
agent("""
what app was I last using? open it again.
""")
# → current_app → launch

docs.title: "Play something chill on Spotify"
docs.text:
agent("launch spotify and play my chill playlist")
# → launch com.spotify.music → smart_tap "chill" → smart_tap play

docs.title: "Read my last HN story"
docs.text:
agent("open news.ycombinator.com in my browser")
# → open_url

docs.title: Common Package Names
docs.text:
App Package WhatsApp com.whatsapp Spotify com.spotify.music Instagram com.instagram.android Telegram org.telegram.messenger Chrome com.android.chrome Gmail com.google.android.gm Maps com.google.android.apps.maps GoogleCamera com.google.android.GoogleCamera Settings com.android.settings Phone com.google.android.dialer

Or just ask the agent:

agent("list packages matching 'spot' and launch the music one")

docs.title: What's Next
docs.text:
• Smart Tap — interact once the app is open
  • Vision — see what's on screen after launching
  • Camera — drive the camera app specifically

docs.title: Camera — Physical Photos & Video
docs.text:
Drive the actual camera. Not screenshots — real photos from the lens.

adb(action="camera_photo", facing="back")
adb(action="camera_photo", facing="front")
adb(action="camera_video", facing="back", duration_sec=10)

Returns the captured media as a Converse API image/video block, so the agent can immediately see what it just captured.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Agent
    participant Tool as adb tool
    participant Cam as GoogleCamera
    participant FS as /sdcard/DCIM

    Agent->>Tool: camera_photo, facing="front"
    Tool->>Tool: note latest DCIM file
    Tool->>Cam: am start STILL_IMAGE_CAMERA
    Note over Cam: viewfinder up
    Tool->>Cam: smart_tap "Switch to front camera"
    Tool->>Cam: tap shutter_button (by resource-id)
    Cam->>FS: write IMG_xxx.jpg
    Tool->>FS: poll for new file
    FS-->>Tool: /sdcard/DCIM/Camera/IMG_xxx.jpg
    Tool->>Tool: adb pull → /tmp/IMG_xxx.jpg
    Tool-->>Agent: image block (JPEG bytes)

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Param Default Notes facing "back" "back" or "front" output_path /tmp/<original>.jpg Where to pull the file auto_pull True Pull to host after capture include_image True Embed image block in response return_base64 False Also include base64 timeout_sec 15 Max wait for file to appear (Night Sight needs ~5s)

docs.title: Strategy: Why GoogleCamera?
docs.text:
Most phones have multiple camera apps. We target GoogleCamera specifically because:

  • Its shutter button has a stable resource-id: com.google.android.GoogleCamera:id/shutter_button
  • Google doesn't rename it across versions
  • Available on all Pixel / most Android-12+ devices

For other camera apps, adapt via resource_id parameter.

docs.title: Night Sight / Portrait / Other Modes
docs.text:
camera_photo currently uses whatever mode GoogleCamera was last in. To force a mode, smart_tap it first:

adb(action="launch", package="com.google.android.GoogleCamera")
adb(action="smart_tap", text="Night Sight")
adb(action="camera_photo", facing="back")  # captures in Night Sight

docs.title: Video
docs.text:
result = adb(action="camera_video", duration_sec=15, facing="back")
# result["path"] → /tmp/VID_xxx.mp4

For longer-form recording, use screen_record instead (records the screen, not camera).

docs.title: "Take a selfie and tell me how I look"
docs.text:
agent("take a front-facing photo and describe what you see")
# 1. camera_photo facing=front
# 2. vision model sees the photo
# 3. agent describes it

docs.title: "Document what's in front of me"
docs.text: agent("take a back-camera photo and identify any objects")

docs.title: "Is my desk tidy?"
docs.text: agent("take a photo and rate my desk tidiness 1-10")

docs.title: Pitfalls
docs.text: Problem Fix Photo never appears (timeout) Night Sight in low light can take 5-8s — increase timeout_sec Wrong camera faces you Pass facing="front" / "back" explicitly Shutter never fires Camera UI still animating — we retry 3× by default Photo ends up blurry Phone moved during capture — use a stand Permission denied reading DCIM Verify adb shell ls /sdcard/DCIM/Camera works

docs.title: What's Next
docs.text:
• Vision — difference between screenshots and camera
  • Sensors — ambient light, gyro (auto-select mode)
  • Smart Tap — how we find the shutter button

docs.title: DevDuck Integration
docs.text: DevDuck is the minimalist, self-adapting agent runtime. strands-adb is a first-class tool drop-in — one env var, no code.

docs.title: 1-Line Setup
docs.text:
pip install devduck strands-adb
export DEVDUCK_TOOLS="strands_adb:adb;strands_tools:shell"
devduck "take a screenshot of my phone"

That's it. DevDuck auto-loads adb from strands_adb, adds shell from strands_tools, and you have a phone-aware agent.

docs.title: The DEVDUCK_TOOLS Format
docs.text:
DEVDUCK_TOOLS="package1:tool1,tool2;package2:tool3"
  • ; separates packages
  • , separates tools in the same package
  • : separates package name from tools

docs.title: Recommended configurations
docs.text:
Minimal phone agentPhone + vision + tasksFull autonomous phone agent 
export DEVDUCK_TOOLS="strands_adb:adb;strands_tools:shell"

export DEVDUCK_TOOLS="strands_adb:adb;strands_tools:shell,file_read,file_write;devduck.tools:tasks,scheduler,notify"

export DEVDUCK_TOOLS="strands_adb:adb;strands_tools:shell,file_read,file_write,use_agent;devduck.tools:tasks,scheduler,ambient_mode,notify,telegram,system_prompt"

docs.title: Persistent via service
docs.text:
Install as a systemd / launchd service that auto-restarts:

devduck service install \
  --name phone-agent \
  --tools "strands_adb:adb;strands_tools:shell" \
  --startup-prompt "monitor notifications, text me on telegram for anything urgent" \
  --env TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=xxx

Now DevDuck runs in the background 24/7 on that host, watching the phone.

→ DevDuck service docs

docs.title: Logcat → Event Bus
docs.text:
DevDuck has an internal event bus. strands-adb pushes logcat events into it:

# In DevDuck:
adb(action="log_stream_start",
    filter="NotificationManagerService",
    topic="phone.notifications")

Every new notification appears in the agent's context automatically on the next query.

graph LR
    PHONE["📱 Phone"] -->|logcat| ADB["strands_adb"]
    ADB -->|publish| BUS["🚌 DevDuck event_bus"]
    BUS -->|inject context| AGENT["🤖 DevDuck agent"]
    AGENT -->|react| PHONE

    style AGENT fill:#3DDC84,color:#000

docs.title: Ambient Mode
docs.text:
DevDuck's ambient mode runs the agent in a background loop. Perfect for phone monitoring:

export DEVDUCK_AMBIENT_MODE=true
export DEVDUCK_AMBIENT_IDLE_SECONDS=60
devduck "monitor my phone every minute and alert me on anything urgent"

Or autonomous mode:

devduck
# then at the prompt:
🦆 auto
🦆 keep my phone at <30°C; drop brightness when it gets hot

→ DevDuck ambient mode docs

docs.title: Scheduler
docs.text:
Cron-style jobs via scheduler:

# Inside devduck:
scheduler(action="add",
  name="morning-brief",
  schedule="0 8 * * *",
  prompt="read my notifications and summarize what I missed overnight",
  tools="strands_adb.adb,strands_tools.shell")

→ DevDuck scheduler docs

docs.title: Remote Phone, Cloud Agent
docs.text:
DevDuck + wireless adb + SSH tunnel = full remote control:

# on the box with your phone:
ssh -R 5037:localhost:5037 cloud.example.com
adb -a -P 5037 nodaemon server &

# on cloud box:
export DEVDUCK_TOOLS="strands_adb:adb;strands_tools:shell"
devduck "check my phone battery and notifications"

→ SSH / Wireless ADB guide

docs.title: Multi-Device via identity
docs.text:
DevDuck's identity tool lets you spawn parallel agents per device:

identity(action="fan_out", system_knowledge='''[
  {"identity": "pixel-phone",   "task": "monitor notifications"},
  {"identity": "samsung-tablet", "task": "keep brightness adaptive"}
]''')

Each identity runs in parallel with its own ADB_SERIAL.

docs.title: Example: Complete Setup
docs.text:
#!/bin/bash
# ~/.devduck_phone_agent.sh
export MODEL_PROVIDER=bedrock
export STRANDS_MODEL_ID="global.anthropic.claude-sonnet-4"
export AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK=xxx
export ADB_SERIAL="192.168.1.42:5555"
export DEVDUCK_TOOLS="strands_adb:adb;strands_tools:shell,file_read,file_write;devduck.tools:tasks,scheduler,ambient_mode,notify,telegram,system_prompt"
export DEVDUCK_AMBIENT_MODE=true
export TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=xxx

devduck "$@"

docs.title: What's Next
docs.text:
• Examples — full DevDuck flows
  • SSH / Wireless ADB — remote setups
  • Safety — production hardening

docs.title: Logcat — Event Streaming
docs.text: logcat is Android's system-wide event log. Every notification, every app error, every broadcast — all streamed from the kernel in real time. strands-adb turns this into agent-consumable events.

docs.title: One-Shot Dump
docs.text:
result = adb(action="logcat", lines=200)
# → last 200 lines of logcat -d

Filter by tag:

adb(action="logcat", filter="WhatsApp", lines=100)
adb(action="logcat", filter="NotificationManagerService:V *:S")

docs.title: Continuous Streaming → Event Bus
docs.text:
This is the killer feature. Start a background thread that tails logcat and pushes events to DevDuck's event bus:

adb(action="log_stream_start",
    filter="NotificationManagerService",
    topic="phone.notification")

Now, inside DevDuck, the event bus receives a new event every time a notification is posted. The agent's context is automatically updated.

# Check stream status
adb(action="log_stream_status")
# → running=True, buffered=47 events, started 3m ago

# Stop
adb(action="log_stream_stop")

graph LR
    PHONE["📱 Phone"] -->|adb logcat -v| STREAM["🔁 background thread"]
    STREAM -->|parse| EVENTS["📨 Events"]
    EVENTS -->|publish| BUS["🚌 DevDuck event_bus"]
    BUS -->|inject into context| AGENT["🤖 Agent"]

    style PHONE fill:#3DDC84,color:#000
    style AGENT fill:#fbbf24,color:#000

docs.title: Parsed Notifications
docs.text:
Raw logcat is noisy. For notifications specifically, use the parsed action:

adb(action="notifications_parsed")
# → [
#   {"app": "WhatsApp", "title": "Mom", "text": "on my way", "time": ...},
#   {"app": "Slack", "title": "#general", "text": "PR ready", "time": ...},
#   ...
# ]

Or raw dump:

adb(action="notifications")

docs.title: Filters
docs.text:
Standard logcat filter syntax works:

adb(action="log_stream_start",
    filter="ActivityManager:I NotificationManagerService:V *:S")
# → Show INFO+ from ActivityManager, VERBOSE+ from NotificationManager, silence everything else

docs.title: Notification triage
docs.text:
adb(action="log_stream_start",
    filter="NotificationManagerService",
    topic="phone.notifications")

# agent loop sees new notifications in context automatically
agent("""
any new important notifications? reply to mom if she messaged.
""")

docs.title: App crash monitoring
docs.text:
adb(action="log_stream_start",
    filter="AndroidRuntime:E *:S",
    topic="phone.crashes")

# agent is notified of every app crash

docs.title: "What's been happening?"
docs.text:
agent("""
what events have happened on my phone in the last 5 minutes?
summarize by app.
""")

docs.title: Performance
docs.text: Streaming logcat is cheap — tens of events/sec, fits easily in memory. The background thread parses lines into structured events and buffers the last N (default 500) in memory, publishing each to the event bus.

docs.title: Privacy
docs.text:
Logcat contains potentially sensitive data: SMS previews, notification content, app state. Treat the event stream like you would any other sensitive telemetry:

  • Don't log it to public places
  • Filter aggressively (filter= narrows scope)
  • Stop the stream when you're done

docs.title: What's Next
docs.text:
• DevDuck Integration — event bus details
  • Sensors — combine logcat + sensor events
  • Notification Triage example — full flow

docs.title: Safety
docs.text: strands-adb can do almost anything on the phone. That's the feature. With great power…

docs.title: The Threat Model
docs.text:
Your agent can:

  • Read everything on the screen
  • Tap any UI element
  • Install / uninstall apps
  • Mutate system settings (airplane, ringer, brightness)
  • Send SMS / open dialer
  • Drain battery (camera, recording)
  • Leak data (push files off device)

An overly-helpful LLM or an adversarial prompt can misuse this. Defend in depth.

docs.title: 1. Allowlists
docs.text:
Restrict which packages the agent can install/uninstall:

ALLOWED_INSTALL = {"com.mycompany.testapp"}
ALLOWED_UNINSTALL = {"com.spam.bloatware"}

# In your wrapper / middleware:
if action == "uninstall" and package not in ALLOWED_UNINSTALL:
    raise PermissionError(f"uninstall of {package} not allowed")

docs.title: 2. Dry-Run Mode
docs.text:
For destructive actions, preview before committing:

adb(action="setting_put",
    namespace="global", key="airplane_mode_on", value="1",
    dry_run=True)
# → shows what WOULD happen, doesn't apply

Works for: install, uninstall, clear_data, setting_put, set_airplane_mode, set_bluetooth.

docs.title: 3. No Plaintext Secrets
docs.text:
• Never store PIN/password in code / env in plaintext.
  • unlock takes pin= at call time — passed once, not persisted.
  • Consider a secrets manager (AWS Secrets Manager, 1Password CLI, macOS Keychain) for anything long-lived.

docs.title: 4. Snapshot + Restore
docs.text:
Before a session that touches settings:

snapshot = adb(action="setting_dump", namespace="system")

try:
    # agent does stuff
    agent("optimize phone for movie watching")
finally:
    # restore
    for k, v in snapshot["settings"].items():
        adb(action="setting_put", namespace="system", key=k, value=v)

docs.title: 5. Rate Limiting
docs.text:
Hot loops can drain battery and hit adb rate limits. If you're polling:

import time
while True:
    state = adb(action="sensors")
    # ... do stuff
    time.sleep(1)   # at least 1 Hz

docs.title: 6. Don't Expose adb to the Internet
docs.text:
• Never run adb -a -P 5037 nodaemon server on a public IP.
  • SSH tunnel only. Or Tailscale. Or WireGuard.
  • Treat the adb port like a root shell — because it effectively is one.

docs.title: 7. Auditability
docs.text:
Log every action. DevDuck + strands-adb emit to stdout + log files by default. For production:

import logging
logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO, filename="adb_audit.log")

All adb invocations are logged at DEBUG with the full command.

docs.title: 8. Tool Consent
docs.text:
By default DevDuck runs with BYPASS_TOOL_CONSENT=true (no per-tool confirmation). For sensitive deployments, turn it off:

export BYPASS_TOOL_CONSENT=false

The agent will prompt before executing destructive actions.

docs.title: 9. Network Isolation
docs.text: If the phone handles sensitive data, keep it on a VLAN that can only reach your agent host + required internet services.

docs.title: 10. User Intent Confirmation
docs.text:
For anything that sends/spends/deletes:

# Don't just "send the text" — confirm
agent("""
draft a text to Mom saying 'love you'.
DO NOT send it. show it to me and wait for explicit confirmation.
""")

Then the user reviews, then replies "send".

docs.title: Known Limits
docs.text:
strands-adb does not (by design):

  • Store credentials
  • Bypass screen locks without an explicit PIN
  • Enable adb over TCP — user does that
  • Grant itself permissions beyond what adb provides

If you need more, add companion APK capabilities (FRONTIERS.md) — and review each one carefully.

docs.title: What's Next
docs.text:
• DevDuck Integration — production runtime
  • Settings — reversible mutations
  • API Reference — full action signatures

docs.title: Screen Recording
docs.text:
Record what your agent is doing on-device. Critical for debugging UI automation, demos, and after-the-fact review of agent actions.

Three actions, two modes:

Action Mode Use When screen_record Blocking You know the duration upfront (≤180s) screen_record_start Background Agent needs to keep acting while recording screen_record_stop Background Stop + pull + optionally merge segments screen_record_status Background Check elapsed time + segments pulled so far

docs.title: Quick Start — Record While Acting
docs.text:
The common pattern: start recording, perform actions, stop and get the video.

from strands_adb import adb

# 1. Start recording in the background (returns immediately)
adb(
    action="screen_record_start",
    output_path="/tmp/agent_demo.mp4",
    screenrec_bit_rate_mbps=4,       # 4 Mbps (default)
    screenrec_size="720x1600",       # downscale (None = native)
    screenrec_segment_sec=180,       # max per segment (Android cap)
)

# 2. Do whatever your agent needs to do
adb(action="tap", x=500, y=1000)
adb(action="type", text="hello world")
adb(action="key", key="enter")
# ... minutes of work ...

# 3. Stop, pull segments, optionally merge
result = adb(action="screen_record_stop")
# result["segments"] → list of mp4 paths
# result["merged_path"] → single concatenated mp4 (if ffmpeg available)

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Param Type Default Notes output_path str /tmp/adb_rec_<ts>.mp4 Where the first / final mp4 lands screenrec_bit_rate_mbps int 4 1-20 Mbps. Higher = larger file screenrec_size str None e.g. "720x1280". None = device native screenrec_segment_sec int 180 Per-segment cap. Android hard limit is 180

docs.title: Long Recordings (>3 minutes)
docs.text:
Android screenrecord caps at 180 seconds per invocation. screen_record_start automatically chains segments and pulls each one as soon as it finishes, so stop is fast regardless of how long the recording ran.

If ffmpeg is on your $PATH, segments are concatenated into a single mp4:

# 10-minute recording → 4 segments → auto-merged
adb(action="screen_record_start", output_path="/tmp/long.mp4")
time.sleep(600)
result = adb(action="screen_record_stop")
print(result["merged_path"])    # /tmp/long_merged.mp4
print(len(result["segments"])) # 4

docs.title: Status Polling
docs.text:
status = adb(action="screen_record_status")
# {"running": True, "elapsed_sec": 47.3, "segments": [...], "output_path": "..."}

docs.title: Blocking Variant (fixed duration)
docs.text:
If you know exactly how long you want to record and don't need to do anything else during that window:

adb(action="screen_record", duration_sec=10, output_path="/tmp/ten_seconds.mp4")
# Blocks for 10s, then pulls the mp4

docs.title: File Size Reference
docs.text:
Typical bit-rates and per-minute file sizes (at 720×1600):

Bit Rate ~MB per minute Use Case 1 Mbps 7 MB Audit logs, low-bandwidth 2 Mbps 15 MB Demo recordings 4 Mbps (default) 30 MB General purpose 8 Mbps 60 MB High-motion content

docs.title: Known Limitations
docs.text:
• DRM / Secure windows (banking apps, Netflix, etc.) record as black. This is enforced by Android at the kernel level — no workaround.
  • Audio is NOT recorded. screenrecord is video-only.
  • Segment seams may show a ~1 frame hiccup at the 180s boundary. ffmpeg -c copy concat preserves streams but doesn't re-encode the seam.
  • Max bit-rate on most devices caps around 20 Mbps regardless of what you pass.

docs.title: Record every agent run
docs.text:
Wrap your agent loop:

import time
from strands_adb import adb

rec_path = f"/tmp/agent_{int(time.time())}.mp4"
adb(action="screen_record_start", output_path=rec_path)
try:
    agent("book a haircut appointment")
finally:
    result = adb(action="screen_record_stop")
    print(f"Recording: {result.get('merged_path') or rec_path}")

docs.title: Record only on failure
docs.text:
adb(action="screen_record_start", output_path="/tmp/pending.mp4")
try:
    agent.do_work()
    # Success — discard recording
    adb(action="screen_record_stop")
    os.remove("/tmp/pending.mp4")
except Exception as e:
    # Failure — keep the evidence
    result = adb(action="screen_record_stop")
    print(f"Failure recorded: {result['merged_path']}")
    raise

docs.title: Pair with logcat streaming
docs.text:
adb(action="log_stream_start", log_filters=["ActivityTaskManager:I", "*:S"])
adb(action="screen_record_start", output_path="/tmp/combo.mp4")

# agent work ...

adb(action="screen_record_stop")
adb(action="log_stream_stop")
# Now you have video + structured logs for the same window

docs.title: Sensors & Thermals
docs.text: The phone is a sensor platform: accelerometer, gyro, light, proximity, temperature, battery. All accessible via adb dumpsys.

docs.title: sensors
docs.text:
One-shot snapshot of all sensor values:

result = adb(action="sensors")
# → accelerometer (m/s²): x=0.1, y=9.7, z=0.3  (phone is upright)
# → gyroscope (rad/s):    x=0.0, y=0.0, z=0.0  (not rotating)
# → ambient light (lux):  450                    (indoor)
# → proximity (cm):       5                      (nothing nearby)
# → magnetic field:       ...
# → pressure (hPa):       1012                   (sea level-ish)
# → step counter:         14382                  (since last reboot)

docs.title: thermals
docs.text:
CPU, skin, battery, modem temperatures:

adb(action="thermals")
# → CPU:     67.2 °C  (warm, not throttling)
# → Skin:    35.8 °C  (comfortable to hold)
# → Battery: 32.1 °C  (normal)
# → GPU:     58.0 °C
# → Modem:   41.3 °C

Great for: - Detecting throttling before performance degrades - Warning the user when the phone is overheating - Monitoring during long video recording / ML inference

docs.title: battery
docs.text:
adb(action="battery")
# → level: 87%, charging: true, temp: 32.1°C, health: good, USB power source

docs.title: wifi_info
docs.text:
adb(action="wifi_info")
# → SSID: "HomeNet", BSSID: "aa:bb:...", signal: -52 dBm, freq: 5180 MHz

docs.title: Phone state classification
docs.text:
agent("is my phone face-down on the desk?")
# Agent:
#   sensors → accelerometer z ≈ -9.8 m/s² → face-down

docs.title: Thermal budgeting
docs.text:
agent("""
check thermals every 30s for 5 minutes.
alert me if CPU > 85°C.
""")

docs.title: Motion detection
docs.text:
agent("""
take a sensors reading now, wait 10s, take another.
did the phone move?
""")
# compares gyro + accelerometer deltas

docs.title: Ambient-light-aware UX
docs.text:
agent("""
if ambient light < 10 lux, enable dark mode and
drop brightness to 20%.
""")
# → sensors → set_brightness → setting_put dark mode

docs.title: Limitations
docs.text:
• sensors is a snapshot, not a stream. For continuous data, poll at 1 Hz from your agent loop.
  • For high-frequency streams (200 Hz accelerometer) you need a companion APK. See FRONTIERS.md #1.
  • Not all sensors are on all devices. Pixels have barometric pressure; many Samsungs don't.

docs.title: What's Next
docs.text:
• Settings — mutate device state based on sensor readings
  • Logcat — stream events for a continuous event loop
  • DevDuck — scheduled polling + event bus

docs.title: Settings Mutation
docs.text: Programmatically change device state: brightness, ringer, airplane mode, bluetooth, any setting Android exposes.

docs.title: High-Level Actions
docs.text:
adb(action="set_brightness", value=128)       # 0-255
adb(action="set_ringer", mode="silent")       # silent | vibrate | normal
adb(action="set_airplane_mode", enabled=True)
adb(action="set_bluetooth", enabled=False)

docs.title: Low-Level Settings API
docs.text:
Android stores settings in three namespaces:

Namespace Purpose global Device-wide (wifi, airplane, time) system User prefs (brightness, volume, ringtone) secure Security-sensitive (location, accessibility)

docs.title: Get / Put / Delete
docs.text:
adb(action="setting_get", namespace="global", key="airplane_mode_on")
adb(action="setting_put", namespace="system", key="screen_brightness", value="128")
adb(action="setting_delete", namespace="secure", key="my_key")

docs.title: List All in Namespace
docs.text:
adb(action="setting_list", namespace="global")
# → huge list of all global settings

docs.title: Dump
docs.text: adb(action="setting_dump", namespace="system")

docs.title: Common Keys
docs.text: Key Namespace Values airplane_mode_on global 0 / 1 wifi_on global 0 / 1 bluetooth_on global 0 / 1 auto_time global 0 / 1 screen_brightness system 0–255 screen_brightness_mode system 0 manual, 1 auto screen_off_timeout system milliseconds vibrate_on system 0 / 1 notification_sound system URI location_mode secure 0 off, 3 high accuracy accessibility_enabled secure 0 / 1

docs.title: "Going to sleep"
docs.text:
agent("""
airplane mode on, ringer silent, brightness 10%,
screen timeout 30s. I'm going to bed.
""")

docs.title: "Meeting mode"
docs.text: agent("ringer to vibrate, do-not-disturb on, keep wifi")

docs.title: "Travel mode"
docs.text: agent("enable airplane, then turn wifi back on for gate wifi")

docs.title: "Dark room adaptation"
docs.text:
agent("""
if ambient light is below 5 lux,
drop brightness to 20 and enable dark theme.
""")

docs.title: Permission Caveats
docs.text:
Some settings require extra permissions:

  • secure namespace may need WRITE_SECURE_SETTINGS granted via adb: 
    adb shell pm grant <pkg> android.permission.WRITE_SECURE_SETTINGS
  • Do Not Disturb requires a notification policy exception.
  • Default SMS/Dialer changes need user confirmation.

Most toggles in global and system work out of the box.

docs.title: Safety
docs.text:
Settings mutation can soft-brick UX in rare cases (e.g. brightness=0 + auto-mode off = black screen). strands-adb ships:

  • Dry-run mode via dry_run=True on destructive actions
  • Reverse snapshots — setting_dump before + apply + diff after
# Snapshot
before = adb(action="setting_dump", namespace="system")

# Mutate
adb(action="set_brightness", value=10)

# Restore later
for key, value in before["settings"].items():
    adb(action="setting_put", namespace="system", key=key, value=value)

docs.title: What's Next
docs.text:
• Sensors — read state, then mutate
  • Accessibility — a11y-specific settings
  • Safety — allowlists, dry-run, reverse ops

docs.title: Smart Tap — Semantic UI Automation
docs.text:
smart_tap is the "just do the thing" action. Give it text, it finds the UI element, taps it.

adb(action="smart_tap", text="Send")
adb(action="smart_tap", text="Settings")
adb(action="smart_tap", content_desc="More options")

No pixel coordinates. No fragile hardcoded bounds. Works across Android versions, device sizes, and app updates.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
graph LR
    IN["action=smart_tap<br/>text='Send'"] --> DUMP["uiautomator dump"]
    DUMP --> PARSE["Parse XML tree"]
    PARSE --> MATCH{"Match by:<br/>text / content-desc<br/>/ resource-id"}
    MATCH --> BOUNDS["Extract bounds [x1,y1][x2,y2]"]
    BOUNDS --> CENTER["Center = ((x1+x2)/2, (y1+y2)/2)"]
    CENTER --> TAP["input tap cx cy"]

    style IN fill:#3DDC84,color:#000
    style TAP fill:#3DDC84,color:#000

docs.title: Matching Strategies
docs.text:
smart_tap tries multiple strategies in priority order:

Priority Strategy Matches against 1 Exact text= Button labels, list items 2 Exact content-desc= Accessibility labels (great for icon-only buttons) 3 Exact resource-id= Developer-specified IDs (most stable) 4 Partial (contains) Fallback when exact fails

Pass whichever field you know:

adb(action="smart_tap", text="Sign in")
adb(action="smart_tap", content_desc="Switch to front camera")
adb(action="smart_tap", resource_id="com.whatsapp:id/send")

docs.title: Tap WhatsApp send button
docs.text:
adb(action="launch", package="com.whatsapp")
adb(action="smart_tap", text="Message")        # open a chat
adb(action="type_text", text="on my way")
adb(action="smart_tap", content_desc="Send")

docs.title: Dismiss a dialog
docs.text:
adb(action="smart_tap", text="OK")
# or
adb(action="smart_tap", text="Dismiss")

docs.title: Camera shutter
docs.text:
# The shutter is an icon-only button — text match won't work
adb(action="smart_tap",
    resource_id="com.google.android.GoogleCamera:id/shutter_button")

docs.title: Pairing with Vision
docs.text:
The most powerful pattern: vision identifies what to tap, smart_tap does it.

agent("""
take a screenshot, identify any blue call-to-action button,
then smart_tap it by its label text
""")

Flow:

sequenceDiagram
    participant Agent
    participant Tool as adb tool
    participant Phone

    Agent->>Tool: screenshot
    Tool->>Phone: screencap
    Phone-->>Tool: PNG
    Tool-->>Agent: image block
    Note over Agent: Vision: "the button says 'Get started'"
    Agent->>Tool: smart_tap text="Get started"
    Tool->>Phone: uiautomator dump → find → tap
    Phone-->>Tool: ✅

docs.title: Waiting for Elements
docs.text:
Dialogs appear asynchronously. Use ui_wait_for:

# Wait up to 10s for the "Accept" button to appear
adb(action="ui_wait_for", text="Accept", timeout_sec=10)
adb(action="smart_tap", text="Accept")

docs.title: When smart_tap Fails
docs.text:
1. Element in a WebView / Compose / Flutter — may not expose via UIAutomator. Fall back to vision + tap with coordinates.
  2. Animation in progress — add a small time.sleep(0.5) or ui_wait_for.
  3. Element scrolled off-screen — scroll first (swipe up), then smart_tap.
  4. Duplicate matches — use resource_id or content_desc for uniqueness.

Debug by dumping the tree:

adb(action="ui_dump")
# Returns full XML — inspect what's actually there

docs.title: Lower-Level: ui_find + ui_tap_by
docs.text:
For complex criteria:

# Find all nodes matching criteria
adb(action="ui_find", text="Reply", clickable=True)

# Tap by compound criteria
adb(action="ui_tap_by", text="Reply", index=0, clickable=True)

docs.title: What's Next
docs.text:
• UI Automation — raw ui_find, ui_dump, ui_wait_for
  • Vision — screenshot ↔ tap pairing
  • Examples — real flows end-to-end

docs.title: SSH / Wireless ADB
docs.text: Run your agent on one machine, control a phone that lives somewhere else.

docs.title: Use Cases
docs.text:
• Your DevDuck lives on a Jetson / VPS / cloud box
  • Your phone is on a desk, on a different network, or in a different room
  • You want no-cable phone control (after initial pairing)

docs.title: Wireless ADB (same network)
docs.text:
Fast, zero-overhead. Android 11+ has native wireless debugging.

# On phone: Settings → Developer options → Wireless debugging → enable
# Tap "Pair device with pairing code"
adb pair 192.168.1.42:41337
# enter 6-digit code from phone

adb connect 192.168.1.42:5555
adb devices
# 192.168.1.42:5555  device

Persist:

echo 'alias phone="adb connect 192.168.1.42:5555"' >> ~/.zshrc

docs.title: SSH Tunnel (remote network)
docs.text: Phone at home, agent in the cloud:

docs.title: On home machine (with phone connected via USB/wireless):
docs.text:
# Run adb server listening on all interfaces
adb -a -P 5037 nodaemon server &

docs.title: From remote agent host:
docs.text:
ssh -L 5037:localhost:5037 user@home-machine.example.com
# in that SSH session:
adb devices    # sees the phone connected at home

Your DevDuck → localhost:5037 → tunneled → home-machine:5037 → actual phone.

docs.title: Termux: adb on the phone itself
docs.text:
Install Termux on the phone, then:

# in Termux
pkg update
pkg install android-tools openssh
sshd
# set a password: passwd

# enable USB debugging + adb over tcpip
adb tcpip 5555
adb connect localhost:5555

SSH into the phone's Termux from anywhere, run adb commands against localhost.

docs.title: Fleet: One Agent, Many Phones
docs.text:
# agent.py
import os
from strands import Agent
from strands_adb import adb

phones = {
    "home":   "192.168.1.42:5555",
    "office": "10.0.0.5:5555",
    "lab":    "192.168.5.12:5555",
}

agent = Agent(tools=[adb])

for name, serial in phones.items():
    os.environ["ADB_SERIAL"] = serial
    agent(f"take a screenshot of {name} and report screen state")

docs.title: Security Notes
docs.text:
• Never expose adb server to the internet without a tunnel. Anyone who can reach :5037 can root the phone.
  • SSH keys > passwords for any remote adb setup.
  • Wireless debugging should be off when you're not using it.
  • Pairing codes are one-time. Unpair old hosts in Developer Options.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: Symptom Fix connection refused Wireless debugging is off, or wrong port 5555 not working Try the port from Wireless debugging page (varies) Drops after phone suspend Normal — reconnect on demand or keep phone awake Works via USB, fails wireless Check both are on the same wifi (no AP isolation) SSH tunnel works but no devices Run adb kill-server && adb start-server after tunnel is up

docs.title: What's Next
docs.text:
• Connect a Device — basics
  • DevDuck Integration — remote agent setup
  • Safety — production best practices

docs.title: UI Automation
docs.text: Low-level UI introspection + interaction. If smart_tap isn't doing what you need, drop down to these.

docs.title: The UIAutomator Tree
docs.text:
Android exposes every on-screen element via uiautomator dump. Each node has:

  • text — visible label
  • resource-id — developer-assigned ID
  • content-desc — accessibility label
  • class — widget type (Button, EditText, ImageView, ...)
  • bounds — [x1,y1][x2,y2] rectangle
  • clickable, focusable, enabled, checked

docs.title: ui_dump
docs.text:
Raw XML of the current screen:

result = adb(action="ui_dump")
# result["content"][0]["text"] = "<hierarchy>...</hierarchy>"

Use for debugging when smart_tap misses.

docs.title: ui_find
docs.text:
Find matching nodes without tapping:

adb(action="ui_find", text="Send")
adb(action="ui_find", resource_id="com.whatsapp:id/send")
adb(action="ui_find", content_desc="More options")
adb(action="ui_find", clickable=True)   # all clickable elements

Returns list of {text, bounds, resource_id, content_desc, clickable, ...}.

docs.title: ui_tap_by
docs.text:
Tap by compound criteria (what smart_tap builds on):

adb(action="ui_tap_by", text="Send", clickable=True)
adb(action="ui_tap_by", resource_id="com.foo:id/bar", index=0)

docs.title: ui_wait_for
docs.text:
Poll until a node appears, return when found or timeout:

adb(action="ui_wait_for", text="Accept", timeout_sec=10)

docs.title: Simple gestures
docs.text:
adb(action="tap", x=500, y=1500)
adb(action="swipe", x1=500, y1=2000, x2=500, y2=500, duration_ms=300)
adb(action="gesture_long_press", x=500, y=500, duration_ms=800)

docs.title: Multi-point paths
docs.text: adb(action="gesture_path", points=[(100, 500), (300, 500), (300, 800)], duration_ms=500)

docs.title: Pinch
docs.text: adb(action="gesture_pinch", cx=500, cy=1000, start_radius=200, end_radius=500)

docs.title: Gesture streams
docs.text:
For complex multi-touch (drawing, zooming, scrolling):

adb(action="gesture_stream", events=[...])

docs.title: Text Input
docs.text:
adb(action="type_text", text="Hello world")
adb(action="key", key="enter")
adb(action="key", key="KEYCODE_BACK")

Key aliases built in: back, home, recent, enter, tab, space, delete, escape, volume_up, volume_down, power, menu, search.

docs.title: Scrolling
docs.text:
Android exposes no native "scroll" — you swipe:

# Scroll down (content moves up)
adb(action="swipe", x1=500, y1=1800, x2=500, y2=600, duration_ms=250)

# Scroll up
adb(action="swipe", x1=500, y1=600, x2=500, y2=1800, duration_ms=250)

# Horizontal scroll
adb(action="swipe", x1=900, y1=1000, x2=100, y2=1000, duration_ms=250)

docs.title: Debug Loop
docs.text:
When building automation flows:

# 1. Dump current state
print(adb(action="ui_dump"))

# 2. Take a screenshot to cross-reference
adb(action="screenshot")

# 3. Try a smart_tap
adb(action="smart_tap", text="Next")

# 4. Verify with another screenshot
adb(action="screenshot")

docs.title: Common Pitfalls
docs.text: Pitfall Fix Node exists in screenshot but not in ui_dump Likely WebView / Compose. Use vision + pixel tap. Animation mid-motion, dump catches wrong state ui_wait_for or sleep briefly Multiple matches for same text Use resource_id or index=N bounds=[0,0][0,0] Element is off-screen — scroll first Dump is stale Call ui_dump fresh, don't cache

docs.title: What's Next
docs.text:
• Smart Tap — the high-level wrapper
  • Vision — fall back to pixels when UIAutomator fails
  • Examples — real automation flows

docs.title: Vision — Screenshots as Image Blocks
docs.text:
The single most important feature of strands-adb is this: the agent can see the screen.

Unlike naive adb wrappers that return a file path and force the LLM to "imagine" what's there, screenshot returns a proper Converse API image block — the same shape that strands_tools.image_reader uses. The vision model receives the actual pixels.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Agent
    participant Tool as adb tool
    participant Phone

    Agent->>Tool: action="screenshot"
    Tool->>Phone: adb exec-out screencap -p
    Phone-->>Tool: PNG bytes
    Tool-->>Agent: content:[<br/>  {"text": "screenshot saved..."},<br/>  {"image": {"format": "png", "source": {"bytes": ...}}}<br/>]
    Note over Agent: Vision model sees pixels directly

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
from strands import Agent
from strands_adb import adb

agent = Agent(tools=[adb])
agent("take a screenshot and tell me what app is open")

Direct tool call:

result = adb(action="screenshot")
# result["path"]         → "/tmp/adb_screenshot_1730000000.png"
# result["size_bytes"]   → 284512
# result["content"][0]   → {"text": "screenshot saved: /tmp/...png (284512 bytes)"}
# result["content"][1]   → {"image": {"format": "png", "source": {"bytes": b"\x89PNG..."}}}

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Param Default Notes output_path /tmp/adb_screenshot_<ts>.png Where to save the PNG locally serial $ADB_SERIAL Target specific device include_image True Embed Converse image block (disable to save context tokens) return_base64 False Also include base64 in response

docs.title: Performance
docs.text:
Screenshot via adb exec-out screencap -p is the fast path — direct binary pipe, no intermediate file on device. Typical timings on a Pixel 10 Pro over USB:

Method Time exec-out screencap -p (default) 180–350 ms Fallback: shell screencap + adb pull 700–1200 ms

The fallback triggers automatically if exec-out fails (some devices have flaky exec-out support).

docs.title: When to Disable include_image
docs.text:
By default every screenshot pushes ~300 KB of PNG bytes into the context window. That's fine for a few calls, but if you're in a long-running ambient loop:

# Just want the path, don't clutter context
adb(action="screenshot", include_image=False)

docs.title: Vision + UI Inspection
docs.text:
Screenshots pair beautifully with ui_dump for hybrid strategies:

agent("""
take a screenshot, identify the WhatsApp compose button,
then tell me its bounds so we can tap it
""")
# Agent:
#   1. screenshot → sees the UI
#   2. ui_dump    → gets the XML with bounds
#   3. correlates → reports bounds of the button it just saw

For full automation use smart_tap which does all of this in one call.

docs.title: Wake First on Locked Screen
docs.text:
A locked screen screenshots to pure black. Wake + unlock first:

adb(action="wake")
adb(action="unlock", pin="1234")
adb(action="screenshot")

Or just tell the agent:

agent("wake up my phone and take a screenshot")

docs.title: Recording
docs.text:
For screen recording (not just stills), use screen_record:

adb(action="screen_record", duration_sec=30, output_path="/tmp/session.mp4")

Or extract frames at N fps:

adb(action="screen_frames", duration_sec=10, fps=2, output_dir="/tmp/frames/")

Each frame returns as an image block, perfect for video-capable models like strands-cosmos.

docs.title: What's Next
docs.text:
• Smart Tap — one-shot semantic UI interaction
  • UI Automation — ui_find, ui_wait_for, XML dumps
  • Camera — physical camera (not just screen)
